Amended Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Complaint Anti- Slapp Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 425.16
Off Calendar Nov-21-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT ELENA ASTURIAS AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION AND SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT ANTI- SLAPP PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 425.16 is DENIED. The Court notes that the original Anti-SLAPP was filed (July 7, 2023) more than 60 days after personal service of the Summons and Complaint (April 24, 2023). Additional time passed between the lifting of the bankruptcy stay and the filing of the operative Amended Notice. The Court has discretion to deny the motion on that basis alone. CCP 425.16(f).
The Court further notes that no courtesy copies of the Amended Notice or prior filings have been delivered to the Court in violation of SFRL 2.7B and Court's November 7, 2023 minute order. (Copies of opposition papers received). This failure is an independent reason for the Court to deny the motion in its discretion.
Given the amount of work performed by the Plaintiff (see opposition) the Court will rule on the merits and DENY the motion. Under the standard articulated in Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal.5th 376, 384-385 ("We have described this second step as a "summary-judgment-like procedure." The court does not weigh evidence or resolve conflicting factual claims. Its inquiry is limited to whether the plaintiff has stated a legally sufficient claim and made prima facie factual showing sufficient to sustain a favorable judgment. It accepts the plaintiff's evidence as true, and evaluates the defendant's showing only to determine if it defeats the plaintiff's claim as a matter of law. " [C]laims with the requisite minimal merit may proceed." ), the Court finds that the Plaintiff met its burden under the second prong of CCP 425.16 to show probability of prevailing on the merits on its cause of action for malicious prosecution: that the Underlying Lawsuit (1) was commenced by or at the direction of the defendant and was pursued to a legal termination in plaintiffs', favor; (2) was brought [or continued] without probable cause ; and (3) was initiated with malice. Bertero v. National General Corp., (1974) 13 Cal.3d 43.
As evidence submitted by the Defendant may only be evaluated "to determine if it defeats the plaintiff's claims as a matter or law," the Court reviewed all evidence/ declarations submitted by the defendants and finds that none of them defeat the claims as a matter of law, instead they create factual dispute that cannot be resolved on an Anti-Slapp motion under Baral.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
